title: Sarcomelicope leiocarpa
intro: Sarcomelicope leiocarpa, also known as the Pua tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, colds, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Sarcomelicope leiocarpa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Pacific Island medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of a poultice or ointment to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Sarcomelicope leiocarpa is classified within the family Rutaceae, which is commonly known as the citrus family. This classification places Sarcomelicope leiocarpa alongside other plants such as citrus and orange species. The genus Sarcomelicope is native to Australia and the Pacific Islands, and it contains several species of flowering plants. Sarcomelicope leiocarpa is a specific species within this genus. Further classification of Sarcomelicope leiocarpa can be found in the APG III system, which categorizes it as a member of the order Sapindales.
